content.summary: Utility module providing path helpers, property creation, parameter serialization, layout tools, menu helpers, and a tScript bridge. Available as 'import TDFunctions' in any DAT.
warnings: createProperty adds a class-level property shared across ALL instances of the extension class — use me.store for instance-specific data when replication is involved.
tScript() is slow (creates/destroys a temporary DAT per call) — always prefer the Python API when available.
getShortcutPath may return None — always validate the result with op() before using.

members.name: getShortcutPath
members.description: Best-guess resolution of a shortcut path. Always validate the returned path with op().

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: Value to return if shortcut not found

examples.code:
path = TDFunctions.getShortcutPath('MOPs')
target = op(path) if path else None
if target is None:
    debug('shortcut not found')
examples.label: Safe usage with validation

warnings: Returns None if shortcut not found — always null-check before passing to op()

members.name: parentLevel
members.description: Returns the ancestor OP at depth levels up, not the depth itself.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: Number of levels to go up

members.name: createProperty
members.description: Adds a Python property to type(ext) — the class, not the instance. The property is shared across all instances of the extension class.

parameters.name: readOnly
parameters.description: If True, property has no setter

parameters.name: dependable
parameters.description: If True, wraps value for TD dependency tracking

warnings: Creates class-level property shared across ALL instances — use me.store for instance-specific data when replication is involved

members.name: makeDeepDependable
members.description: Wraps an existing property's value in DependDict/DependList recursively. Only use when downstream operators must cook on nested changes.

members.name: applyParInfo
members.description: Applies parameter info dict to a COMP. Returns names of parameters that failed — empty list means success.

examples.label: Check return value for failures

warnings: Always check the return value — typos in parameter names will silently fail

members.name: getParInfo
members.description: Returns a serializable dict of parameter values matching the pattern.

members.name: bindChain
members.description: Returns the full chain of bound parameters from source to final.

members.name: findNetworkEdges
members.description: Returns {'left': x, 'right': x, 'top': y, 'bottom': y} of child node bounds.

members.name: arrangeNode
members.description: Positions a node relative to siblings.

members.name: parMenu
members.description: Returns a dict suitable for par.menuNames/par.menuLabels. Convenience wrapper — same as building the lists manually.

parameters.name: menuLabels
parameters.description: Menu item labels (uses menuNames if None)

members.name: tScript
members.description: Executes a tscript command by creating a temporary textDAT, running the command, reading stdout, and deleting the DAT.

examples.code:
# Bad — slow in a loop
for i in range(100):
    result = TDFunctions.tScript(f'opfind -n /project1 -t chopexecDAT')

# Good — use Python API instead
results = op('/project1').findChildren(type=chopexecDAT)
examples.label: Prefer Python API over tScript

warnings: Slow — creates/destroys a temporary DAT per call. If the Python API can do it, never use tScript()
